name: CasADi (Symbolic Framework for Optimal Control)
pricing_notes: Open source (LGPL-3.0); KU Leuven / Joel Andersson

performance.notes: C++ core; automatic differentiation (AD) via graph coloring; JIT compilation via LLVM

agent_readiness.notes: Symbolic framework for optimal control and numerical optimization with exact AD. from casadi import *. x = MX.sym('x'). f = x**2 + sin(x). grad_f = gradient(f, x). J = jacobian(f, x). nlp = {'x': x, 'f': f, 'g': g}. solver = nlpsol('solver', 'ipopt', nlp). sol = solver(lbg=lb, ubg=ub). Automatic differentiation: forward/reverse mode. Integrators for ODE/DAE. JIT compilation. Interface to IPOPT, SNOPT, Bonmin. Used in robotics, autonomous vehicles, MPC. MATLAB interface also available.

gotchas: Steep learning curve — symbolic programming different from NumPy
LGPL-3.0 license requires dynamic linking for commercial use
C++ compilation step required for JIT-optimized code
Error messages from symbolic expressions can be cryptic
best_when: Optimal control with exact automatic differentiation (robotics, vehicles)
Research requiring exact Jacobians/Hessians of complex functions
avoid_when: Standard LP/MIP optimization (OR-Tools or PuLP much simpler)
Teams without optimal control / symbolic math background
When PyTorch/JAX AD is sufficient (more Pythonic)
summary: CasADi is a symbolic framework for optimal control with automatic differentiation. MX.sym() creates symbolic variables; f = x**2 + sin(x); gradient(f, x) computes exact gradient. nlpsol('solver', 'ipopt', nlp) for NLP. Integrators for ODE/DAE systems. JIT compilation via LLVM. Used in robotics (autonomous vehicles, MPC). LGPL-3.0. Steep learning curve but uniquely powerful for large-scale optimal control requiring exact sparse Jacobians. Python and MATLAB interfaces.
